name: azure-ai-projects-ts
description: Build AI applications using Azure AI Projects SDK for JavaScript (@azure/ai-projects). Use when working with Foundry project clients, agents, connections, deployments, datasets, indexes, evaluations, or getting OpenAI clients.
capabilities: Create and manage AI agents with various tool types including code interpreter and file search
Configure Azure AI connections for accessing external resources securely
List and filter model deployments by publisher and type
Upload and manage datasets for training and evaluation workflows
Create and manage search indexes for retrieval-augmented generation
Access OpenAI client for chat completions and conversation management
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer produced false positives by misinterpreting markdown code block delimiters (```) as shell commands. The file contains only documentation and code examples for the official Azure AI Projects SDK. URLs are placeholder examples with template values. Environment variable access follows standard best practices. No executable code or security risks present.
valueStatement: Azure AI Projects SDK simplifies building AI applications with agents, connections, and evaluations. This skill provides comprehensive TypeScript examples for Azure AI Foundry development.
userTitle: Build AI Apps with Azure AI Projects SDK
limitations: Requires Azure AI project endpoint and valid Azure credentials
Does not execute code - provides documentation and examples only
Requires npm packages @azure/ai-projects and @azure/identity to be installed

useCases.title: Enterprise AI Agent Development
useCases.description: Build production-ready AI agents with code interpreter, file search, and web search capabilities for enterprise workflows.

useCases.targetUser: Data engineers implementing knowledge base solutions
useCases.title: RAG Application Setup
useCases.description: Configure Azure AI Search indexes and agents for retrieval-augmented generation with custom document collections.

useCases.targetUser: ML engineers monitoring and optimizing AI systems
useCases.title: AI Evaluation Pipeline
useCases.description: Set up evaluators and datasets to measure and improve AI model performance across different metrics.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Agent Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a basic AI agent using Azure AI Projects SDK that responds to user questions with helpful answers. Include proper authentication and error handling.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner - Getting started with Azure AI agents

promptTemplates.title: Agent with File Search
promptTemplates.prompt: Build an AI agent that can search through uploaded documents using Azure AI Search. Include steps for creating the index, uploading files, and configuring the agent with file search tools.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Tool Agent Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an AI agent that combines code interpreter, file search, and custom function tools. Show how to handle tool execution and response aggregation.

promptTemplates.title: Evaluation Pipeline Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up a complete evaluation pipeline using Azure AI evaluators. Include dataset upload, metric configuration, and result analysis for measuring agent performance.
promptTemplates.scenario: Expert - Production AI system monitoring and optimization

bestPractices: Use getOpenAIClient() method for chat completions, conversations, and file operations
Version your agents using createVersion for reproducible and trackable deployments
Clean up resources by deleting agents and conversations when no longer needed
Store credentials in environment variables and use connections for secure access
Filter deployments by modelPublisher to find specific model types efficiently
antiPatterns: Hardcoding credentials instead of using environment variables or connections
Creating agents without versioning, making rollback and tracking difficult
Leaving conversations and agent versions active after use, accumulating costs
Skipping error handling for network operations and API calls

faq.question: What Azure resources do I need to use this SDK?
faq.answer: You need an Azure AI project in Azure AI Foundry. This requires an Azure subscription and access to Azure AI services in your region.

faq.question: How does authentication work with Azure AI Projects?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential for automatic authentication. It supports Azure CLI, managed identities, and service principals. Set AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT environment variable.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without Azure subscription?
faq.answer: No. The Azure AI Projects SDK requires an active Azure subscription and Azure AI project. Free tier options may be available for testing.

faq.question: What is the difference between agents and OpenAI client?
faq.answer: Agents provide higher-level abstractions with built-in tools and memory. OpenAI client gives direct access to chat completions and lower-level API features.

faq.question: How do I add custom tools to my agents?
faq.answer: Define function tools with name, description, and JSON schema parameters. Register them when creating the agent version using the tools array.

faq.question: Can this skill execute code on my system?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides documentation and code examples only. Code interpreter tools run in Azure's sandboxed environment, not on your local system.
